title: [$250] Always show Update credentials for Sage Intacct connections

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Currently, the "Enter credentials" / "Update credentials" option in the accounting connection overflow menu is only shown when there's been a sync error due to incorrect credentials. This is controlled by the condition at [`PolicyAccountingPage.tsx:113`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/b4103e0062b3f68c9603257c024baa65f669e57b/src/pages/workspace/accounting/PolicyAccountingPage.tsx#L113): ```typescript const shouldShowEnterCredentials = connectedIntegration && !!synchronizationError && isAuthenticationError(policy, connectedIntegration); ``` This requires `lastSync.isAuthenticationError === true` ([`connections/index.ts:263`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/b4103e0062b3f68c9603257c024baa65f669e57b/src/libs/actions/connections/index.ts#L263)), meaning users can only update credentials reactively after they break — not proactively. While this makes sense for OAuth-based connections (e.g., QuickBooks, Xero) and NetSuite tokens, Sage Intacct uses a Web Services user with a company ID, user ID, and password. Users should be able to proactively update these credentials (e.g., during a planned password rotation) without waiting for a sync failure. ## Proposed Change For Sage Intacct connections, always show the "Update credentials" option in the overflow menu regardless of whether there's been an authentication error. The "Sync Now" option should still be available alongside it. **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C03U7DCU4/p1778544534188309

title: [$250] Workspace - Categories page crashes with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'isSelected')" when typing/clearing/retyping search with categories selected

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven't already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** N/A **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** **Slack conversation:** [Slack thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778499219516519) ## Action Performed: 1. Open a workspace with many imported categories (e.g. QuickBooks Online with 50+ categories) at Settings > Workspaces > [workspace] > Categories. 2. Select 2 or more categories using their checkboxes. 3. Click into the "Find category" search bar and type a query (e.g. \`re\`). The list filters down. 4. Clear the search by removing all characters. 5. Quickly type a different query (e.g. \`ad\`) within ~300 ms of clearing. ## Expected Result: The list re-filters to match the new query. No crash. ## Actual Result: The page goes blank and the browser console reports: \`\`\` TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'isSelected') at renderItem (authScreens.prefetch-*.bundle.js) at useMemo (vendors-*.bundle.js) ... \`\`\` ## Workaround: Wait roughly half a second between clearing the search and typing again, or de-select all categories before searching. Users can still browse and edit categories without selection. ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos
Root cause analysis The crash is in [\`BaseSelectionList.renderItem\`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/SelectionList/BaseSelectionList.tsx#L349) → [\`isItemSelected\`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/SelectionList/BaseSelectionList.tsx#L118-L121), which dereferences \`item.isSelected\` while \`item\` is \`undefined\`. FlashList v2 (\`@shopify/flash-list@2.3.0\`) hands \`renderItem\` a \`{item: undefined, index: }\` because the array it received from [\`SelectionListWithModal\`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/SelectionListWithModal/index.tsx#L50-L67) is internally inconsistent: - \`displayData = isFiltering ? debouncedData : filteredData\` - \`isFiltering\` is decided by \`filteredData.length < debouncedData.length\` only. When the user clears the search and re-types within the 300 ms (\`CONST.TIMING.SEARCH_OPTION_LIST_DEBOUNCE_TIME\`) debounce window, \`debouncedData\` still contains items from the previous query while \`filteredData\` is the new one. The length-only check returns true, FlashList is handed the stale array, sees data shrink-grow-shrink in <100 ms, and a recycled cell ends up indexed past the new data bounds. Two fixes needed: 1. Defensive \`if (!item) return null\` guard plus optional chaining in \`isItemSelected\` to make the renderer resilient to FlashList recycler quirks. 2. Replace the length-only \`isFiltering\` heuristic in \`SelectionListWithModal\` with a key-set superset check so \`debouncedData\` is only used when it's actually a less-filtered view of the same query. Related (likely same root cause, different surface): #89588.
